/// Production gRPC Server.
/// Provides high-speed, structured communication based on HTTP/2 and Protobuf.

use tonic::{transport::Server, Request, Response, Status};

// Mock definition for the service (usually generated by Prost)
mod proto {
    tonic::include_proto!("fusion_service");
    // Placeholder includes for structs
    // pub struct RequestStatus; pub struct ResponseStatus;
}

pub struct FusionGrpcServer;

#[tonic::async_trait]
impl proto::fusion_service_server::FusionService for FusionGrpcServer {
    // Example: Execute a remote tensor operation
    async fn execute_tensor_op(
        &self,
        request: Request<proto::TensorRequest>,
    ) -> Result<Response<proto::TensorResponse>, Status> {
        // 1. Deserialize Protobuf -> Fusion Types
        // 2. Execute fusion_core::ops::matmul(...)
        // 3. Serialize Fusion Type -> Protobuf Response

        println!("Received gRPC request for tensor op: {:?}", request.into_inner().op_name);
        
        let reply = proto::TensorResponse { 
            op_name: "MatMul Complete".into(),
            // result_tensor: Some(...)
        };
        Ok(Response::new(reply))
    }
}

pub async fn run_grpc_server(addr: SocketAddr) -> StdResult<()> {
    let svc = proto::fusion_service_server::FusionServiceServer::new(FusionGrpcServer);
    
    // Tonic uses its own underlying HTTP/2 transport layer
    Server::builder()
        .add_service(svc)
        .serve(addr)
        .await
        .map_err(|e| StdError::Core(fusion_core::FusionError::CompilationError(format!("gRPC server failed: {}", e))))
}
